paper_focus.title: Excellent mechanical properties and underlined deformation twinning mechanism of a low-carbon low-density cryogenic steel
paper_focus.research_objective: To reveal the microstructure evolution and its effects on mechanical properties of a low-carbon low-density austenitic steel at both ambient and cryogenic temperatures, with particular focus on the deformation twinning mechanism under high stacking fault energy.
material_systems: austenite-based duplex steel with a small amount of ferrite (~3%)
κ-carbide (mentioned as a potential precipitate in high-carbon variants)
main_process_variables: hot rolling to 16.5 mm diameter rods
solution treatment at 1050°C for 2 h followed by oil quenching
tensile testing at room temperature (RT) and liquid nitrogen temperature (LNT)
Charpy V-notch impact testing at RT and LNT
